note_data.title: Research methods
keyPoints: The biological approach states that humans are biological organisms made up of physiological processes, so all thoughts and cognitive processes must be biological in origin
Heritability coefficients quantify the genetic basis of characteristics - IQ has a heritability coefficient of 0.5 according to Plomin
Genotype is an individual's genetic make-up, while phenotype is the physical expression of their genotype
Two people may have the same genotype but different phenotypes due to personal choices or epigenetics
Natural selection is the mechanism of evolution where genetically-determined behaviours with selective advantage are passed to future generations
The biological approach has practical applications in drug development, such as dopamine antagonists like Chlorpromazine for schizophrenia
Biological determinism suggests all behaviour is caused by internal biological forces over which we have no control

definitions.definition: The study of the influence of an organism's genetic composition on its behaviour and the interaction of heredity and environment insofar as they affect behaviour
An individual's genetic make-up, where a gene is a short section of DNA coding for specific proteins
The physical expression of an individual's genotype
A change in gene expression, without altering an individual's genetic make-up
The mechanism of evolution where genetically-determined behaviour giving selective advantage increases chances of surviving and reproducing
The process by which organisms change over time as a result of changes in heritable physical or behavioural traits

howToAnswer.instruction: Name the research method/sampling technique. One word answers acceptable.
Give details about what the method involves. Use 'involves' or 'includes' to structure your answer.
Give both strengths AND weaknesses. Use 'However' to show balance between points.
Propose a realistic research method with brief justification of why it's appropriate.

modelAnswer.question: Explain one strength and one weakness of using questionnaires in psychological research. [4 marks]
modelAnswer.answer: One strength of questionnaires is that they can collect data from large numbers of participants quickly and cost-effectively. This allows researchers to gather a representative sample and generalize findings to the wider population. One weakness is that questionnaires rely on self-report which may lack validity. Participants may give socially desirable responses rather than honest answers, especially on sensitive topics, which reduces the accuracy of the data collected.
markBreakdown: Mark 2: Elaboration of strength with justification
Mark 4: Elaboration of weakness with justification

commonMistakes: Confusing genotype and phenotype - remember genotype is genetic make-up while phenotype is physical expression
Assuming twin studies can clearly separate nature from nurture effects
Overlooking that MZ twins may share more similar environments than DZ twins, not just genes
examinerTips: Use specific examples like Chlorpromazine as a dopamine antagonist when discussing practical applications
Consider real-world implications of biological determinism for the judicial system and economy in evaluation points
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Draw a simple experimental design diagram showing: IV (what's manipulated) → DV (what's measured), with arrows indicating cause and effect relationship. Include control group and experimental group boxes.
pastPaperRefs: This topic was tested in M/J 2023 P2 Q4 (6 marks) — they asked students to evaluate the advantages and disadvantages of using questionnaires compared to interviews in psychological research
In O/N 2022 P1 Q2 (4 marks) — candidates had to identify and explain two ethical considerations when conducting research with human participants
M/J 2022 P2 Q1 (8 marks) — students were required to design a simple experiment including identification of variables, sampling method, and one control measure
O/N 2023 P1 Q5 (3 marks) — asked for definition and one advantage of using random sampling in psychological research

workedExample.question: A psychologist wants to investigate whether listening to music affects concentration levels in students. Design a simple experiment to test this hypothesis. In your answer, identify the independent variable, dependent variable, and describe one control measure you would use. (6 marks)
steps: Step 1: Identify the independent variable (IV) - what the researcher manipulates
Step 2: Identify the dependent variable (DV) - what the researcher measures
Step 3: Choose an appropriate control measure to ensure fair testing
Step 4: Structure answer clearly with proper psychological terminology
workedExample.answer: Independent variable: Whether students listen to music or not (music condition vs no music condition). Dependent variable: Concentration levels, which could be measured using a concentration test score or time taken to complete a standardized task. Control measure: All participants should complete the same concentration task in the same environment (same room, same time of day, same lighting) to ensure that any differences in concentration are due to the music and not other environmental factors.
workedExample.markScheme: 1 mark for correctly identifying IV, 1 mark for correctly identifying DV, 1 mark for suggesting how to measure DV, 2 marks for appropriate control measure, 1 mark for clear explanation of why the control is necessary

examinerWarnings: Examiner Report 2023: 'Many candidates confused correlation with causation when discussing research findings' — always emphasize that correlation does not prove cause and effect when evaluating correlational studies

Examiner Report 2022: 'Students frequently failed to provide enough detail when describing sampling methods' — always explain HOW the sampling method works, not just name it (e.g., for random sampling, explain using names from a hat or random number generator)

Examiner Report 2023: 'Weak answers on ethical considerations often just listed guidelines without application' — always link ethical considerations to the specific research scenario in the question rather than giving generic definitions
note_data.keyDiagram: Research Methods Flow Chart: Draw a flowchart showing the research process from top to bottom: 1) Research Question/Hypothesis at top, 2) Choose Method (branching to Experiment, Survey, Observation, Case Study), 3) Select Participants (showing different sampling methods), 4) Collect Data, 5) Analyze Results, 6) Draw Conclusions. Include arrows showing the flow and add 'Ethical Considerations' as a box connected to every stage. This diagram helps visualize the complete research process and shows how different elements connect.
note_data.quickRecap: The biological approach views all behaviour as stemming from biological processes including genes, hormones and neurotransmitters. It emphasizes the interaction between genotype and phenotype, with natural selection driving evolution of beneficial traits. While offering practical applications in drug development, it raises concerns about biological determinism and its implications for moral responsibility.
